text: When software doesn't work as expected, bugs are the usual cause. A systematic approach helps you find and fix them effectively. Start by reproducing the problem consistently, then identify the bug by isolating the faulty code. Use debugging tools like breakpoints in your editor to pause execution and inspect variables. Another useful technique is logging: adding print statements to monitor what your program does step by step. This can reveal hidden errors. Finally, test your fix thoroughly before moving on. Remember, patience is key. With practice, debugging becomes a manageable and even satisfying puzzle.
